SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.automation

Class AutomationHub

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.automation.AutomationHub
All Implemented Interfaces:
IAutomationHub, MessageBusClientListener, Runnable
public class AutomationHub extends Object implements IAutomationHub, MessageBusClientListener
Concrete implementation of IAutomationHub.

Threading model

  Netty receiver thread                 AutomationHub main thread
  ─────────────────────                 ─────────────────────────
  MessageBusClientRunner receives       blocks on inboundQueue.take()
  inbound frame, deserialises it,  →    dispatches by topic:
  calls enqueue(message)                  EVENT     → handleEvent()
                                          AUTOMATION → handleAutomationMessage()

Rule storage

Rules are loaded from StateStore on start() so the hub is immediately ready without waiting for the coordinator to replay them. Subsequent ADD/REMOVE/TOGGLE operations arrive via the AUTOMATION topic and are applied to both the in-memory list and the state store atomically.

  • Constructor Details

    • AutomationHub

      public AutomationHub(String hubId, String messageBusHost, int messageBusId, StateStore stateStore)
      Constructs the AutomationHub.
      Parameters:
      hubId - unique identifier for this hub on the message bus
      stateStore - persistent storage for loading and saving rules

    • AutomationHub

      public AutomationHub(String hubId, MessageBusClientRunner runner, StateStore state)

  • Method Details

    • onMessage

      public void onMessage(Message<?> message)

      Places the message on the inbound queue to be processed by the hub's dedicated thread, keeping the Netty I/O thread free.

      Specified by:
      onMessage in interface MessageBusClientListener

    • onError

      public void onError(Throwable cause)
      Specified by:
      onError in interface MessageBusClientListener

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts the Automation Hub.

      In order:

      1. Loads all existing rules from StateStore so the hub is immediately ready without waiting for the coordinator to replay rules.
      2. Sends REGISTER and SUBSCRIBE frames for the EVENT and AUTOMATION topics to the Message Bus via MessageBusClientRunner.

      Loads all rules from StateStore into memory, then sends REGISTER and SUBSCRIBE frames for the EVENT and AUTOMATION topics.

      Specified by:
      start in interface IAutomationHub

    • loadRules

      public void loadRules()

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the hub's processing loop. The main thread exits after the current message finishes processing. In-flight messages remaining in the queue are discarded.
      Specified by:
      stop in interface IAutomationHub

    • enqueue

      public void enqueue(Message<?> message)
      Places an inbound message on the processing queue. Called by the Netty pipeline handler on the receiver thread — returns immediately so the I/O thread is never blocked.
      Specified by:
      enqueue in interface IAutomationHub
      Parameters:
      message - the message received from the Message Bus

    • run

      public void run()
      Main processing loop. Blocks on the inbound queue and dispatches each message by topic:
      • TopicType.EVENT → IAutomationHub.handleEvent(DeviceEvent)
      • TopicType.AUTOMATION → IAutomationHub.handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage)
      Exits when IAutomationHub.stop() is called or the thread is interrupted.

      Blocks on inboundQueue.take() when idle — no busy-waiting. Dispatches each message by its TopicType:

      • EVENT → casts payload to DeviceEvent, calls handleEvent(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.DeviceEvent)
      • AUTOMATION → casts payload to AutomationRuleMessage, calls handleAutomationMessage(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRuleMessage)
      • anything else → logged and discarded
      Specified by:
      run in interface IAutomationHub
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable

    • addRule

      public boolean addRule(AutomationRule rule)
      Adds a new automation rule to the in-memory registry. Called by IAutomationHub.handleAutomationMessage(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRuleMessage) for ADD operations.
      Specified by:
      addRule in interface IAutomationHub
      Parameters:
      rule - the rule to add; ignored if null

    • removeRule

      public boolean removeRule(String ruleId)
      Removes an existing automation rule by ID. Has no effect if no rule with the given ID exists. Called by IAutomationHub.handleAutomationMessage(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRuleMessage) for REMOVE operations.
      Specified by:
      removeRule in interface IAutomationHub
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to remove

    • toggleRule

      public boolean toggleRule(String ruleId, boolean active)
      Enables or disables an existing rule without removing it. Called by IAutomationHub.handleAutomationMessage(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRuleMessage) for TOGGLE operations.
      Specified by:
      toggleRule in interface IAutomationHub
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to update
      active - true to activate, false to deactivate

    • getAllRules

      public List<AutomationRule> getAllRules()
      Returns all rules currently held by this hub, including inactive ones.
      Specified by:
      getAllRules in interface IAutomationHub
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable snapshot of the current rule list

    • setLeader

      public void setLeader(boolean leader)

    • handleEvent

      public void handleEvent(DeviceEvent event)
      Evaluates a DeviceEvent against all active rules and publishes a Command to the COMMAND topic for each match.

      A rule matches when all three conditions hold:

      1. rule.triggerDeviceType == event.sourceDeviceType
      2. rule.triggerEventType.equals(event.eventType)
      3. rule.triggerDeviceId == null (wildcard) or rule.triggerDeviceId.equals(event.sourceDeviceId)

      Iterates the rule list once. For each active matching rule, constructs a Command and publishes it to the COMMAND topic. Matching is O(n) over the rule list — acceptable given the expected scale (hundreds of rules at most).

      Specified by:
      handleEvent in interface IAutomationHub
      Parameters:
      event - the device event to evaluate

    • handleAutomationMessage

      public boolean handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage message)
      Applies an automation rule operation (ADD, REMOVE, or TOGGLE) received from the AUTOMATION topic.

      Delegates to IAutomationHub.addRule(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRule), IAutomationHub.removeRule(java.lang.String), or IAutomationHub.toggleRule(java.lang.String, boolean) based on AutomationRuleMessage.getOperation().

      Delegates to addRule(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRule), removeRule(java.lang.String), or toggleRule(java.lang.String, boolean) based on the operation type. Each delegate method also persists the change to StateStore.

      Specified by:
      handleAutomationMessage in interface IAutomationHub
      Parameters:
      message - the automation rule operation to apply

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Entry point for a standalone Automation Hub. Usage: java AutomationHub hubId busHost busPort dbPath
